name: Aztechnology Payroll Server
description: Mid-tier corporate payroll host. Orange security. Good for testing sheaf triggers, IC combat, and file operations.
gmNotes: Test run for dev/QA. Designed to exercise: Probe/Killer/Blaster/Crippler IC, full sheaf sequence, file operations, slaves, and dump shock paths.

hostGMNotes.host-azt-payroll-main: Main payroll host. Orange security. Sheaf hits passive at 3, active at 8, shutdown at 15. IC load is intentionally varied — Probe first to detect the decker, then proactive IC if alert escalates.
hostGMNotes.host-azt-backup-san: Backup SAN node linking to main payroll. One-way only — decker can't come back through this route.

hosts.name: AZT-PAY-03 // Payroll Processing
hosts.description: A sterile white server room rendered in clean corporate geometry. Aztechnology corporate logos float as watermarks across every surface. Data streams flow in ordered columns.
hosts.metaphor: Corporate server room — white tile floors, glowing blue data conduits, Aztechnology pyramid motifs

securitySheaf.gmNotes: Passive alert — Probe IC activates. Decker has been noticed but not confirmed.
Additional Scout IC joins — security is building a trace.
Active alert — Killer IC deployed. Security has confirmed intrusion. Decker is now a target.
Blaster IC joins — security escalating to lethal measures.
Crippler IC — targets Evasion attribute. Combined with Blaster this is dangerous.
Shutdown initiated. No further sheaf events trigger. All active IC remain — system is purging.

files.name: Q2_PAYROLL_DISBURSEMENT.dat
files.description: Quarterly payroll disbursement records. 847 employee entries. Salaries, bonuses, department codes. Useful for identifying key personnel or as leverage.
files.gmNotes: Classic payroll data. Could be used to ID corp employees or sold to competitors.

files.name: HR_PERSONNEL_Q2.arc
files.description: Full HR dossiers — home addresses, SIN numbers, biometric data, medical flags for 847 employees. Highly sensitive.
files.gmNotes: Data bomb rating 5 — decker needs to defuse or decrypt carefully. This is the crown jewel.

files.name: PHYS_SECURITY_ROTA.txt
files.description: Physical security rotation schedule for the Aztechnology Seattle campus. Guard posts, shift times, patrol routes for the next 30 days.
files.gmNotes: Good for the Street Sam or Face — not matrix paydata but useful for the crew.

files.name: EXEC_COMMS_ARCHIVE.enc
files.description: Encrypted executive communications archive. Subject lines visible: merger talks, project QUETZAL references, legal exposure memos.
files.gmNotes: Project QUETZAL is a hook for future runs — don't reveal full contents unless player decrypts.

files.name: SYSAUDIT_LOG_CURRENT.log
files.description: Current system audit log. Contains decker's own intrusion trail — editing this would reduce Security Tally.
files.gmNotes: If edited, reduce security tally by 1d6 as a narrative reward — decker cleaned their tracks.

slaves.name: Lobby Security Cameras (x12)
slaves.description: Twelve-camera array covering all lobby access points. Live feeds routed to on-site security station.
slaves.controlEffect: Decker gains live view of all lobby cameras. Can loop footage or disable specific angles. Physical security blind for duration.
slaves.gmNotes: Disabling gives the crew a 10-minute window before a guard physically checks the cameras.

slaves.name: Server Room Door Lock
slaves.description: Mag-lock on the primary server room. Requires keycard + biometric.
slaves.controlEffect: Decker can unlock/lock remotely. Can hold open, hold closed, or trigger a false alarm to draw security away.
slaves.gmNotes: Opening this gives the Rigger or Street Sam direct physical access to the server hardware.

slaves.name: Building Alarm System
slaves.description: Central alarm routing. Connects to Lone Star response contract — 4-minute response time.
slaves.controlEffect: Decker can suppress alarms, trigger false alarms elsewhere in the building, or lock out physical override for 1d6 minutes.
slaves.gmNotes: Data bomb on the alarm slave — designed to punish casual intrusion. Triggers Lone Star dispatch on detonation.

hosts.name: AZT-PAY-03 // Backup SAN
hosts.description: A narrow transit corridor — bare conduit, no ornamentation. One-way data shunt linking the payroll host to the broader Aztechnology regional network.
hosts.metaphor: Bare maintenance corridor — exposed conduit, flickering overhead lights
